title: Killing Minima Ghosts & Owning the Stack
content:
## 🎯 Goal

Stabilize the blog setup by fully removing leftover theme dependencies, fixing broken assumptions, and taking **full ownership** of the Jekyll stack.
## ✅ What I Did

### 🧹 Theme Cleanup & Debugging

- Found an error in `head.html` pointing to a missing `assets/main.scss`
    
- Realized this was **legacy Minima theme residue**
    
- Confirmed I had already switched to a **fully static theme**
    
- Removed / fixed references that assumed Minima was still present
    
- Verified that styling issues were **CSS-related**, not Jekyll-related

📸 Screenshot showing the setup and error during debugging:
![[Screenshot 2026-01-26 at 11.33.34.png]]

### 🗂️ Structure & Organization

- Reorganized folders to reflect actual intent
    
- Created a dedicated `blog/` folder for daily entries only
    
- Cleaned up misplaced files
    
- Fixed navigation order explicitly (no auto-magic)

### 📡 Syndication

- Successfully created a working **RSS feed (`feed.xml`)**
    
- Verified it only includes blog posts
    
- Confirmed it’s compatible with static hosting (GitHub Pages)

## 🧠 What Clicked

### 4️⃣ Why this is actually a win

This wasn’t “wasted time.”  
This was **foundational**.

You did what most beginners never do:

- You **removed magic**
    
- You **understand every file**
    
- You **control your stack**
    

This is how engineers work.  
This is how pentesters think.

## 🧠 New Mental Model (Burn This In)

- **Jekyll** = static site generator
    
- **Theme** = optional convenience
    
- **Static theme** = _your responsibility_
    
- *_Broken CSS ≠ broken Jekyll_
    

Once you internalize this, debugging becomes mechanical—not emotional.

## 🧾 Commits — Jan 26, 2026

- 🔧 **Experimenting with RSS**
    
    - [`experimenting with feed.rss page`](https://github.com/JuriBuora/JuriBuora.github.io/commit/5bd8fe554feb909c5ea4b0cc07e8c2c1b58e68aa)
        
- ✅ **Reached stable state**
    
    - [`finally where i wanted to be`](https://github.com/JuriBuora/JuriBuora.github.io/commit/ba63e14e129eb07fc38b60e3706cc9d1bf0c6a96)
        
- 🧹 **Cleanup & refactor**
    
    - [`Moved stuff around`](https://github.com/JuriBuora/JuriBuora.github.io/commit/fc351ac227acdd9270fe376565760c2568743af5)
        
    - [`moved files around to be in the right folders`](https://github.com/JuriBuora/JuriBuora.github.io/commit/9349e3e8cd315199cee252279f24aa7d7648b3e5)
        
- 📝 **Content organization**
    
    - [`created new folder "blog" to store daily blog entries only`](https://github.com/JuriBuora/JuriBuora.github.io/commit/4e6028104fea19f2316f512844cf21432153745f)
        
- 🧭 **Navigation & UX**
    
    - [`Fixed the order of the items in the header and organized posts into categories`](https://github.com/JuriBuora/JuriBuora.github.io/commit/f485ace2b641a3297879b637d0023c40bee80005)
        
- 🖼️ **Branding**
    
    - [`modified config, head and header to properly see an image logo in the title`](https://github.com/JuriBuora/JuriBuora.github.io/commit/d2aa2c1fbff56a3ffae999cb9b31c1e98e0facf4)

## 🧠 Key Takeaways

- Theme leftovers are **technical debt**, not harmless clutter
    
- Static sites force you to understand the whole pipeline
    
- RSS is not “extra” — it’s infrastructure
    
- When things break cleanly, you’re doing something right
